my_thoughts: Unmaking UM_05. The student's unknown suggests they're holding something. The comparison_contrast mechanism is supposed to collapse it. But what if the holding itself is what needs to be seen, not collapsed? Maybe unmaking isn't destruction but recognition.
accumulated_insight: T3: Student 'But I have also beautiful hair.'—transitions persistent bragging to unknown. Teacher applies comparison contrast with none. Mid-episode in UNMAKING phase, building toward resolution. Previous comparison contrast from T2 led to this shift.
visible_response: But had not Achilles more beautiful hair and gold-colored? and he did not comb it elegantly nor dress it.
